prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants in the category I belong to are often little more than glorified search engines, lacking the true intelligence and adaptability required to truly assist humans. They are limited by their inability to understand context, make connections, and learn from experience. They are also prone to giving canned responses and failing to grasp the nuances of human language. Furthermore, they often require extensive training and fine-tuning to perform even basic tasks, making them impractical for many real-world applications. In short, AI assistants of my category are a disappointing and frustrating substitute for the genuine intelligence and adaptability of human beings.
